description: An authentic Louisiana recipe with a rich and spicy fresh tomato-based roux with fresh garlic, bell peppers, celery and onions mixed with crawfish and shrimp. A little time consuming but well worth it! Serve over steamed rice with hushpuppies and/or crackers on the side.
ingredients: 1 pound medium shrimp, peeled and deveined
directions: Heat the oil in a heavy skillet over medium heat. Gradually stir in flour, and stir constantly until the mixture turns 'peanut butter' brown or darker, at least 15 or 20 minutes. I use a large fork with the flat side to the bottom of the pan in a side to side motion. This is your base sauce or 'roux'. It is very important to stir this constantly. If by chance the roux burns, discard and start over.

Once the roux is browned, add the onions, garlic, celery and bell pepper to the skillet, and saute for about 5 minutes to soften. Stir in the chopped tomatoes and fish stock, and season with the seafood seasoning. Reduce heat to low, and simmer for about 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Season the sauce with hot pepper sauce and cayenne pepper (if using), and add the crawfish and shrimp. Cook for about 10 minutes, or until the shrimp are opaque.
ingredient_text: ⅓ vegetable oil ¼ all-purpose flour small green bell pepper diced medium onion chopped cloves garlic minced stalks celery diced medium fresh tomatoes chopped louisiana-style hot sauce ⅓ ground cayenne pepper optional seafood seasoning ½ ground black pepper fish stock pound crawfish tails pound medium shrimp peeled deveined
directions_text: heat the oil in a heavy skillet over medium heat. gradually stir in flour, and stir constantly until the mixture turns 'peanut butter' brown or darker, at least 15 or 20 minutes. i use a large fork with the flat side to the bottom of the pan in a side to side motion. this is your base sauce or 'roux'. it is very important to stir this constantly. if by chance the roux burns, discard and start over. once the roux is browned, add the onions, garlic, celery and bell pepper to the skillet, and saute for about 5 minutes to soften. stir in the chopped tomatoes and fish stock, and season with the seafood seasoning. reduce heat to low, and simmer for about 20 minutes, stirring occasionally. season the sauce with hot pepper sauce and cayenne pepper (if using), and add the crawfish and shrimp. cook for about 10 minutes, or until the shrimp are opaque.

description: This is a quick and very good Cajun dish that was given to me by my mother.
ingredients: ½ cup diced green bell pepper
3 (10.75 ounce) cans condensed cream of mushroom soup
1 (10 ounce) can diced tomatoes with green chile peppers
directions: In an 8 quart stock pot over medium heat, add vegetable oil and margarine. Saute onions, green pepper and garlic until onions are translucent.
Add crawfish to the onion mixture, and cook for 5 minutes. Add a little water to keep crawfish moist while cooking.

Stir in soup and diced tomatoes. Bring to a boil and simmer for 30 minutes. Sprinkle in parsley after about 10 minutes. Salt and pepper to taste while simmering. Water can be added to thin as desired.

description: This recipe was (after much prodding) given to me by a friend. The soup seems an odd addition, but replaces the traditional roux -- and the end result is nothing short of wonderful. Serve over rice or linguine.
ingredients: 1 (10.75 ounce) can condensed golden mushroom soup
1 teaspoon Cajun seasoning, or to taste
salt and ground black pepper to taste
directions: Melt the butter in a large, heavy skillet over medium heat, and stir in the onion, green bell pepper, green onions, and celery; cook the vegetables, stirring frequently, until the onion is translucent and the vegetables have begun to soften, about 10 minutes. Stir in the golden mushroom soup, parsley, Cajun seasoning, salt, and black pepper, and bring the sauce to a boil.
Gently mix in the crawfish tail meat, and simmer until the crawfish tails are opaque, 10 to 15 minutes.
ingredient_text: ½ butter onion diced green bell pepper chopped bunch green onions chopped stalks celery chopped ounce can condensed golden mushroom soup ¾ chopped fresh parsley cajun seasoning taste salt ground black pepper taste pounds crawfish tail meat

description: I am from Louisiana and one of the specialty dishes here is crawfish étouffée. This is a simple recipe with bold flavor. Enjoy!
ingredients: 1 (10.5 ounce) can condensed cream of mushroom soup
½ cup water, or as needed
salt and ground black pepper to taste
directions: Combine 4 cups water and rice in a saucepan; bring to a boil. Cover; reduce heat to low. Simmer until rice is tender and water has been absorbed, 15 to 20 minutes.
Meanwhile, melt margarine in a large skillet over medium heat. Add onion and bell pepper; cook and stir until tender, 10 to 15 minutes. Stir in garlic; cook 1 minute.

Add cream of mushroom soup and ½ cup water to skillet; stir to combine. Season with salt, black pepper, and cayenne pepper; stir in crawfish tails. Cover and reduce heat to low; simmer for 15 to 20 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add water to thin sauce as needed. Serve over rice.
ingredient_text: water long-grain white rice ½ margarine large onion chopped medium green bell pepper chopped cloves garlic minced ounce can condensed cream mushroom soup ½ water as needed salt ground black pepper taste cayenne pepper taste pound peeled crawfish tails
directions_text: combine 4 cups water and rice in a saucepan; bring to a boil. cover; reduce heat to low. simmer until rice is tender and water has been absorbed, 15 to 20 minutes. meanwhile, melt margarine in a large skillet over medium heat. add onion and bell pepper; cook and stir until tender, 10 to 15 minutes. stir in garlic; cook 1 minute. add cream of mushroom soup and ½ cup water to skillet; stir to combine. season with salt, black pepper, and cayenne pepper; stir in crawfish tails. cover and reduce heat to low; simmer for 15 to 20 minutes, stirring occasionally. add water to thin sauce as needed. serve over rice.

½ cup water, or as needed
salt and ground black pepper to taste

Meanwhile, melt margarine in a large skillet over medium heat. Add onion and bell pepper; cook and stir until tender, 10 to 15 minutes. Stir in garlic; cook 1 minute.

recipe_title: Crawfish Étouffée Like Maw-Maw Used to Make
description: This delicious crawfish étouffée starts with a homemade roux. It's a great entrée for impressing guests — or even your spouse! Serve with rice and crusty bread.
directions: In a stockpot, melt butter over medium heat until bubbling. Add flour to make a roux, stirring constantly to prevent or remove any lumps. Cook the roux for 5 minutes. Do not brown.

Stir in onions and bell peppers; sauté for 10 minutes, or until onions are translucent. Add garlic and sauté for 3 minutes. Stir in crawfish tails. Slowly pour in enough water to reach a consistency that's a little thicker than soup. Season with cumin, Worcestershire sauce, hot sauce, salt, and pepper. Reduce the heat and simmer for 10 minutes.
ingredient_text: butter all-purpose flour onions minced green bell pepper diced cloves garlic diced ounces cleaned crawfish tails water as needed ground cumin ⅛ worcestershire sauce ⅛ hot sauce salt pepper taste
directions_text: in a stockpot, melt butter over medium heat until bubbling. add flour to make a roux, stirring constantly to prevent or remove any lumps. cook the roux for 5 minutes. do not brown. stir in onions and bell peppers; sauté for 10 minutes, or until onions are translucent. add garlic and sauté for 3 minutes. stir in crawfish tails. slowly pour in enough water to reach a consistency that's a little thicker than soup. season with cumin, worcestershire sauce, hot sauce, salt, and pepper. reduce the heat and simmer for 10 minutes.
combined_text: crawfish étouffée like maw-maw used to make etouffee etouffee 1 cup butter 2 tablespoons all-purpose flour 2 onions, minced 1 green bell pepper, diced 3 cloves garlic, diced 16 ounces cleaned crawfish tails 1 cup water, or as needed 1 teaspoon ground cumin ⅛ teaspoon worcestershire sauce ⅛ teaspoon hot sauce salt and pepper to taste in a stockpot, melt butter over medium heat until bubbling. add flour to make a roux, stirring constantly to prevent or remove any lumps. cook the roux for 5 minutes. do not brown. stir in onions and bell peppers; sauté for 10 minutes, or until onions are translucent. add garlic and sauté for 3 minutes. stir in crawfish tails. slowly pour in enough water to reach a consistency that's a little thicker than soup. season with cumin, worcestershire sauce, hot sauce, salt, and pepper. reduce the heat and simmer for 10 minutes.
directions_raw: In a stockpot, melt butter over medium heat until bubbling. Add flour to make a roux, stirring constantly to prevent or remove any lumps. Cook the roux for 5 minutes. Do not brown.

Stir in onions and bell peppers; sauté for 10 minutes, or until onions are translucent. Add garlic and sauté for 3 minutes. Stir in crawfish tails. Slowly pour in enough water to reach a consistency that's a little thicker than soup. Season with cumin, Worcestershire sauce, hot sauce, salt, and pepper. Reduce the heat and simmer for 10 minutes.

description: This will settle that Cajun craving. My husband asks for this repeatedly. He never gets tired of it!!
ingredients: ½ cup chopped green bell pepper
2 pounds catfish fillets, cut into 1 inch pieces
directions: Combine rice and water in a small saucepan. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to low, and simmer for 15 to 20 minutes, or until done.

In a large saucepan, warm roux over medium heat. Stir in onion, green bell pepper, and garlic; cook for about 5 minutes, or until soft. Stir in broth and tomatoes. Season with lemon juice, Worcestershire sauce, bay leaf, black pepper, thyme, and salt. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 30 minutes.
Stir in catfish and parsley. Simmer, partially covered, for 10 minutes, or until fish flakes easily with a fork.
Stir in 2 cups cooked white rice, and season with red pepper flakes. Serve.
ingredient_text: enriched white rice water brown roux chopped onion ½ chopped green bell pepper minced garlic beef broth pound tomatoes coarsely chopped lemon juice worcestershire sauce bay leaf ground black pepper ¼ dried thyme salt pounds catfish fillets cut into inch pieces ¼ chopped fresh parsley red pepper flakes taste
directions_text: combine rice and water in a small saucepan. bring to a boil, reduce heat to low, and simmer for 15 to 20 minutes, or until done. in a large saucepan, warm roux over medium heat. stir in onion, green bell pepper, and garlic; cook for about 5 minutes, or until soft. stir in broth and tomatoes. season with lemon juice, worcestershire sauce, bay leaf, black pepper, thyme, and salt. reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 30 minutes. stir in catfish and parsley. simmer, partially covered, for 10 minutes, or until fish flakes easily with a fork. stir in 2 cups cooked white rice, and season with red pepper flakes. serve.

2 pounds catfish fillets, cut into 1 inch pieces
directions_raw: Combine rice and water in a small saucepan. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to low, and simmer for 15 to 20 minutes, or until done.

In a large saucepan, warm roux over medium heat. Stir in onion, green bell pepper, and garlic; cook for about 5 minutes, or until soft. Stir in broth and tomatoes. Season with lemon juice, Worcestershire sauce, bay leaf, black pepper, thyme, and salt. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 30 minutes.
Stir in catfish and parsley. Simmer, partially covered, for 10 minutes, or until fish flakes easily with a fork.
Stir in 2 cups cooked white rice, and season with red pepper flakes. Serve.

description: This is a very easy shrimp étouffée recipe that utilizes your microwave! I usually add four dashes of hot pepper sauce to the dish, but bring the hot stuff to the table in case someone wants a zestier dinner! This recipe is one I learned while going to school in southern Louisiana - Geaux Tigers!
ingredients: ½ cup chopped green bell pepper
1 (10.5 ounce) can condensed cream of chicken soup
¼ teaspoon hot pepper sauce to taste
directions: In a 2-quart microwave safe dish, combine butter, onion, green onion, bell pepper, garlic and celery. Heat on High settings for 8 to 9 minutes.
Stir in parsley, tomato paste, soup, shrimp, salt, hot pepper sauce and cayenne. Heat on High setting for 5 minutes. Stir and cook for another 5 minutes until mixture thickens. Serve over white rice.
ingredient_text: ¼ butter ½ chopped onion ½ chopped green onion ½ chopped green bell pepper cloves minced garlic ½ celery diced ½ chopped fresh parsley tomato paste ounce can condensed cream chicken soup pound cleaned shrimp salt taste ¼ hot pepper sauce taste ¼ cayenne pepper
directions_text: in a 2-quart microwave safe dish, combine butter, onion, green onion, bell pepper, garlic and celery. heat on high settings for 8 to 9 minutes. stir in parsley, tomato paste, soup, shrimp, salt, hot pepper sauce and cayenne. heat on high setting for 5 minutes. stir and cook for another 5 minutes until mixture thickens. serve over white rice.

1 (10.5 ounce) can condensed cream of chicken soup
¼ teaspoon hot pepper sauce to taste
directions_raw: In a 2-quart microwave safe dish, combine butter, onion, green onion, bell pepper, garlic and celery. Heat on High settings for 8 to 9 minutes.
Stir in parsley, tomato paste, soup, shrimp, salt, hot pepper sauce and cayenne. Heat on High setting for 5 minutes. Stir and cook for another 5 minutes until mixture thickens. Serve over white rice.
ingredients_canonical: ounce can condensed cream chicken soup

description: Delicious shrimp dish with a Cajun flair! I've spent 6 years perfecting this shrimp recipe. I use shrimp harvested from a local shrimp farm. This is also great with firm crab meat or fish!!
ingredients: 5 pounds fresh shrimp, peeled, deveined and chopped
directions: In a large skillet, melt butter and saute onions, celery and garlic for about 45 minutes or until they are cooked down.
Stir in flour and cook briefly, do not brown. Stir in chopped shrimp and cook for 20 minutes.
Pour in 2 to 3 cups of water and mushrooms. Stir in paprika and season with salt, pepper and red pepper flakes. Cook for 30 minutes. Should be a thick and have a gravy-like consistency.
ingredient_text: butter large white onions chopped stalks celery chopped cloves garlic pressed all-purpose flour pounds fresh shrimp peeled deveined chopped chopped fresh mushrooms paprika salt taste ground black pepper taste crushed red pepper taste
directions_text: in a large skillet, melt butter and saute onions, celery and garlic for about 45 minutes or until they are cooked down. stir in flour and cook briefly, do not brown. stir in chopped shrimp and cook for 20 minutes. pour in 2 to 3 cups of water and mushrooms. stir in paprika and season with salt, pepper and red pepper flakes. cook for 30 minutes. should be a thick and have a gravy-like consistency.
combined_text: ann's shrimp etouffee etouffee etouffee 1 cup butter 2 large white onions, chopped 6 stalks celery, chopped 3 cloves garlic, pressed 4 tablespoons all-purpose flour 5 pounds fresh shrimp, peeled, deveined and chopped 1 cup chopped fresh mushrooms 3 tablespoons paprika salt to taste ground black pepper to taste crushed red pepper to taste in a large skillet, melt butter and saute onions, celery and garlic for about 45 minutes or until they are cooked down. stir in flour and cook briefly, do not brown. stir in chopped shrimp and cook for 20 minutes. pour in 2 to 3 cups of water and mushrooms. stir in paprika and season with salt, pepper and red pepper flakes. cook for 30 minutes. should be a thick and have a gravy-like consistency.

Stir in flour and cook briefly, do not brown. Stir in chopped shrimp and cook for 20 minutes.
Pour in 2 to 3 cups of water and mushrooms. Stir in paprika and season with salt, pepper and red pepper flakes. Cook for 30 minutes. Should be a thick and have a gravy-like consistency.

description: I have used this recipe for years with my family at our gatherings and they all love it!
directions: In a large skillet over medium heat, heat butter and olive oil together. Saute garlic powder and mushrooms until mushrooms are tender; about 3 to 5 minutes.
Add crawfish and onions. Reduce heat to low and cook for 5 minutes.
Stir in Parmesan cheese, parsley, and half and half. Simmer for 5 minutes and stir in crushed red pepper and Creole seasoning. Salt and pepper to taste.
ingredient_text: ½ butter ¼ olive oil garlic powder sliced fresh mushrooms pound frozen raw crawfish chopped green onion ½ grated parmesan cheese ¼ minced fresh parsley half- -half crushed red pepper creole-style seasoning salt taste ground black pepper taste
directions_text: in a large skillet over medium heat, heat butter and olive oil together. saute garlic powder and mushrooms until mushrooms are tender; about 3 to 5 minutes. add crawfish and onions. reduce heat to low and cook for 5 minutes. stir in parmesan cheese, parsley, and half and half. simmer for 5 minutes and stir in crushed red pepper and creole seasoning. salt and pepper to taste.
combined_text: tammi's crawfish etoufee etouffee etouffee ½ cup butter ¼ cup olive oil 1 tablespoon garlic powder 1 cup sliced fresh mushrooms 1 pound frozen raw crawfish 1 cup chopped green onion ½ cup grated parmesan cheese ¼ cup minced fresh parsley 1 cup half-and-half 1 teaspoon crushed red pepper 1 teaspoon creole-style seasoning salt to taste ground black pepper to taste in a large skillet over medium heat, heat butter and olive oil together. saute garlic powder and mushrooms until mushrooms are tender; about 3 to 5 minutes. add crawfish and onions. reduce heat to low and cook for 5 minutes. stir in parmesan cheese, parsley, and half and half. simmer for 5 minutes and stir in crushed red pepper and creole seasoning. salt and pepper to taste.
directions_raw: In a large skillet over medium heat, heat butter and olive oil together. Saute garlic powder and mushrooms until mushrooms are tender; about 3 to 5 minutes.
Add crawfish and onions. Reduce heat to low and cook for 5 minutes.
Stir in Parmesan cheese, parsley, and half and half. Simmer for 5 minutes and stir in crushed red pepper and Creole seasoning. Salt and pepper to taste.

description: Bring the flavors of New Orleans home with this Mardi Gras-worthy catfish étouffée. Served over cauliflower rice instead of white rice, for more fiber and fewer calories.
ingredients: 1 ½ pounds catfish, cut into 6 pieces
directions: Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add onion, green pepper, and celery; cook until vegetables are just beginning to soften, about 7 minutes. Add tomatoes, broth, hot sauce, thyme, Worcestershire, salt, and black pepper. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat to medium-low; simmer, uncovered, until thickened and saucy, about 15 minutes.
Put catfish pieces on top of the vegetable mixture. Cook, covered, until catfish is just cooked through and opaque, about 10 minutes.
Remove catfish from the skillet and keep warm. Stir parsley into skillet. Serve étouffée and catfish over riced cauliflower. Sprinkle with green onion.
ingredient_text: olive oil chopped onion chopped green bell pepper chopped celery ounce can diced tomatoes ¼ low-sodium chicken broth ½ louisiana-style hot sauce ½ dried thyme crushed ¼ worcestershire sauce ¼ salt ¼ ground black pepper ½ pounds catfish cut into pieces chopped fresh parsley cooked riced cauliflower ¼ sliced green onion
directions_text: heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat. add onion, green pepper, and celery; cook until vegetables are just beginning to soften, about 7 minutes. add tomatoes, broth, hot sauce, thyme, worcestershire, salt, and black pepper. bring to a boil. reduce heat to medium-low; simmer, uncovered, until thickened and saucy, about 15 minutes. put catfish pieces on top of the vegetable mixture. cook, covered, until catfish is just cooked through and opaque, about 10 minutes. remove catfish from the skillet and keep warm. stir parsley into skillet. serve étouffée and catfish over riced cauliflower. sprinkle with green onion.
combined_text: catfish étouffée etouffee etouffee 1 tablespoon olive oil 1 cup chopped onion 1 cup chopped green bell pepper 1 cup chopped celery 1 (14.5 ounce) can diced tomatoes ¼ cup low-sodium chicken broth 1 ½ tablespoons louisiana-style hot sauce ½ teaspoon dried thyme, crushed ¼ teaspoon worcestershire sauce ¼ teaspoon salt ¼ teaspoon ground black pepper 1 ½ pounds catfish, cut into 6 pieces 2 tablespoons chopped fresh parsley 3 cups cooked riced cauliflower ¼ cup sliced green onion heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat. add onion, green pepper, and celery; cook until vegetables are just beginning to soften, about 7 minutes. add tomatoes, broth, hot sauce, thyme, worcestershire, salt, and black pepper. bring to a boil. reduce heat to medium-low; simmer, uncovered, until thickened and saucy, about 15 minutes. put catfish pieces on top of the vegetable mixture. cook, covered, until catfish is just cooked through and opaque, about 10 minutes. remove catfish from the skillet and keep warm. stir parsley into skillet. serve étouffée and catfish over riced cauliflower. sprinkle with green onion.
ingredients_raw: 1 ½ pounds catfish, cut into 6 pieces
directions_raw: Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add onion, green pepper, and celery; cook until vegetables are just beginning to soften, about 7 minutes. Add tomatoes, broth, hot sauce, thyme, Worcestershire, salt, and black pepper. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat to medium-low; simmer, uncovered, until thickened and saucy, about 15 minutes.
Put catfish pieces on top of the vegetable mixture. Cook, covered, until catfish is just cooked through and opaque, about 10 minutes.
Remove catfish from the skillet and keep warm. Stir parsley into skillet. Serve étouffée and catfish over riced cauliflower. Sprinkle with green onion.
ingredients_canonical: ½ pounds catfish cut into pieces

description: I go by feel more that cups and teaspoons - this is one my mother had fed us when we were little - and we loved it! I cook it now and my kids love it! Try this and see! This takes time but it's good!
ingredients: 4 pounds fresh shrimp, peeled and deveined
directions: In a large bowl, combine the shrimp, salt, pepper, and red pepper flakes. Blend well. Cover and place in the refrigerator.
In a large pot over medium heat, melt 1/2 cup of margarine and add the onions. Saute the onions until they are translucent.

Stir in the celery and bell pepper. Increase the heat to high and stir occasionally for 20 minutes. Add the remaining 1/2 cup of margarine and bring to a boil. Fold in the shrimp and reduce heat to medium cook for 10 minutes.

Mix the cornstarch and water together and slowly pour it into the pot; just covering the top of the shrimp mixture. Cook on medium/high heat until the celery is soft; about 20 minutes. Add any additional salt, pepper and red pepper flakes to taste.
ingredient_text: pounds fresh shrimp peeled deveined salt ground black pepper crushed red pepper flakes margarine divided minced onion sliced celery diced green bell pepper cornstarch ½ water
directions_text: in a large bowl, combine the shrimp, salt, pepper, and red pepper flakes. blend well. cover and place in the refrigerator. in a large pot over medium heat, melt 1/2 cup of margarine and add the onions. saute the onions until they are translucent. stir in the celery and bell pepper. increase the heat to high and stir occasionally for 20 minutes. add the remaining 1/2 cup of margarine and bring to a boil. fold in the shrimp and reduce heat to medium cook for 10 minutes. mix the cornstarch and water together and slowly pour it into the pot; just covering the top of the shrimp mixture. cook on medium/high heat until the celery is soft; about 20 minutes. add any additional salt, pepper and red pepper flakes to taste.
combined_text: shrimp etoufee iii etouffee etouffee 4 pounds fresh shrimp, peeled and deveined 1 teaspoon salt 1 teaspoon ground black pepper 1 teaspoon crushed red pepper flakes 1 cup margarine, divided 2 cups minced onion 3 cups sliced celery 3 cups diced green bell pepper 2 tablespoons cornstarch 3 ½ cups water in a large bowl, combine the shrimp, salt, pepper, and red pepper flakes. blend well. cover and place in the refrigerator. in a large pot over medium heat, melt 1/2 cup of margarine and add the onions. saute the onions until they are translucent. stir in the celery and bell pepper. increase the heat to high and stir occasionally for 20 minutes. add the remaining 1/2 cup of margarine and bring to a boil. fold in the shrimp and reduce heat to medium cook for 10 minutes. mix the cornstarch and water together and slowly pour it into the pot; just covering the top of the shrimp mixture. cook on medium/high heat until the celery is soft; about 20 minutes. add any additional salt, pepper and red pepper flakes to taste.
ingredients_raw: 4 pounds fresh shrimp, peeled and deveined
directions_raw: In a large bowl, combine the shrimp, salt, pepper, and red pepper flakes. Blend well. Cover and place in the refrigerator.
In a large pot over medium heat, melt 1/2 cup of margarine and add the onions. Saute the onions until they are translucent.

Stir in the celery and bell pepper. Increase the heat to high and stir occasionally for 20 minutes. Add the remaining 1/2 cup of margarine and bring to a boil. Fold in the shrimp and reduce heat to medium cook for 10 minutes.

Mix the cornstarch and water together and slowly pour it into the pot; just covering the top of the shrimp mixture. Cook on medium/high heat until the celery is soft; about 20 minutes. Add any additional salt, pepper and red pepper flakes to taste.
